content.problemStatement:
You want to make a binary string SSS of length NNN. You will be paid according to the following rules:


For each occurrence of the subsequence 000 in SSS, you are paid AAA coins.
For each occurrence of the subsequence 111 in SSS, you are paid BBB coins.
For each occurrence of the subsequence 010101 in SSS, you are paid CCC coins.
For each occurrence of the subsequence 101010 in SSS, you are paid DDD coins.
Note that the occurrence **does not need** to be continuous, i.e. 010101010101 has 333 occurrences of 010101 not 222. Thus, for the string 010101010101 you get 2⋅A+2⋅B+3⋅C+D2 \cdot A + 2 \cdot B + 3 \cdot C + D2⋅A+2⋅B+3⋅C+D coins.

You want to maximize the number of coins you get. Find this maximum value.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first and only line of input contains 555 integers - N,A,B,C,DN, A, B, C, DN,A,B,C,D.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum number of coins you can get.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The optimal string is 101010, which pays 2+1+4=72 + 1 + 4 = 72+1+4=7 coins.
Test Case 2 : The optimal string is 010101010101, which pays 2⋅2+2⋅2+3⋅1+1⋅1=122 \cdot 2 + 2 \cdot 2 + 3 \cdot 1 + 1 \cdot 1 = 122⋅2+2⋅2+3⋅1+1⋅1=12.
